pattern: Split the interior stones into two interleaved paths (even-indexed and odd-indexed) — one used going out, the other coming back — so that no jump ever has to skip more than one stone; the answer is then just the largest gap between a stone and the one two positions ahead of it.
hints: Going and returning must together visit every stone exactly once (except the two ends), so think of it as splitting the stones into two sequences that partition the interior positions.

The best possible split alternates by index parity: one path uses stones at even indices, the other uses stones at odd indices — that way every jump only ever has to cover the gap between a stone and the one two spots later.

So the answer is simply the maximum of stones[i+2] - stones[i] over all valid i; when there are only two stones, the frog must jump directly there and back, so the answer is just stones[1] - stones[0].

edge_inputs.description: exactly two stones — direct jump there and back
five stones with an uneven gap pattern
evenly spaced stones — cost equals twice the spacing
odd number of stones (four), testing the parity split lines up correctly
large uniform gaps near the value ceiling
one huge gap near the start that a naive 'skip every other stone from the front only' rule would miss pairing correctly
a wrong solution using stones[-1]-stones[-2] as the answer would get this wrong since the true bottleneck is an internal two-apart gap

stress.description: large strictly increasing stone list with random gaps, up to 100000 stones

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maxJump(self, stones):
        n = len(stones)
        interior = list(range(1, n - 1))
        best = None
        for mask in range(1 << len(interior)):
            fwd = [0] + [interior[i] for i in range(len(interior)) if mask & (1 << i)] + [n - 1]
            bwd = [0] + [interior[i] for i in range(len(interior)) if not (mask & (1 << i))] + [n - 1]
            fwd_cost = max(stones[fwd[i + 1]] - stones[fwd[i]] for i in range(len(fwd) - 1))
            bwd_cost = max(stones[bwd[i + 1]] - stones[bwd[i]] for i in range(len(bwd) - 1))
            cost = max(fwd_cost, bwd_cost)
            if best is None or cost < best:
                best = cost
        return best
